issue.body:
Filed from the pd-book-tools deep code/security review.

Report: `docs/research/2026-05-22-deep-code-security-review.md`
Finding: #31

Moved from mistakenly filed meta issue: ConcaveTrillion/ocr-container-meta#324

Evidence:

- `Makefile:234`
- `Makefile:240`
- `Makefile:257`
- `Makefile:263`

`HF_LAYOUT_UPSTREAM`, `HF_LAYOUT_FORK`, `HF_LAYOUT_MIRROR`, and `SHA` are interpolated into shell recipes.

Impact:

- If untrusted Make variables are supplied, developer-only targets can be command-injection surfaces.

Remediation:

- Validate variables with strict regexes, or move logic into Python using subprocess argument lists.

---
This issue was filed from a read-only review. Confirm behavior with a focused failing test before implementation where practical.
issue.title: pd-book-tools: developer Makefile targets interpolate untrusted variables
